title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 509.110 - Pleading in alternative — consistency
text:
509.110.  Pleading in alternative — consistency. — A party may set forth two or more statements of a claim or defense alternately or hypothetically, either in one count or defense or in separate counts or defenses.  When two or more statements are made in the alternative and one of them if made independently would be sufficient, the pleading is not made insufficient by the insufficiency of one or more of the alternative statements.  A party may also state as many separate claims or defenses as he has whether based on legal or on equitable grounds or on both.
(L. 1943 p. 353 § 42)
